behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Transitive dependency extraction usually depends on multiple components (manifest parser, lockfile/tree resolver, normalization) and real project files. A unit test on parser logic alone can miss wiring or resolver/file-format failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against fixture repos (e.g., npm with package-lock, pip with requirements+lock) on disk and assert the emitted dependency list includes expected direct and transitive packages with versions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real API request/response semantics for version-range matching. In-process mocks can falsely pass even if query params, encoding, or API contract behavior is wrong.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real/staging vulnerability API with seeded packages covering overlapping names and different vulnerable version ranges; assert only CVEs matching the manifest’s exact version constraints are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic in a local comparator/sort function with no I/O boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Provide an unsorted in-memory CVE list spanning all severities and tied severities with different CVSS values; assert exact sorted order.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur in parser-writer roundtrips (formatting, key order, unrelated field mutation). A pure unit mock can miss file serialization side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Apply bump operation to real manifest fixtures on disk, then diff before/after and assert only the target dependency version changed and all other bytes/semantics remain unchanged per file type expectations.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This crosses real network/API boundaries and repository behavior. Mocked PR clients can hide auth, permission, branch, and diff-upload failures.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test repository, run auto-fix flow and assert a PR is opened, contains exactly one dependency-version change, and targets the expected branch/base.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic with no infrastructure dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-test semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata and edge cases) and assert breaking/non-breaking classification matches semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This validates orchestration between semver decision, PR creation gate, and output/reporting path across modules. Unit tests can miss miswired control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with a fixture requiring major bump, using a local PR client stub plus real report generation modules; assert PR path is not invoked and review-required flag appears in output.
